summary: Jeff Atwood addresses the practical challenge of keeping kids entertained with media on long trips without buying expensive high-capacity iPads for everyone. He recommends the HooToo TripMate Titan, a $40 device that combines a 10400mAh battery with WiFi-based USB storage sharing. The post provides detailed setup instructions including firmware upgrades, storage recommendations, and how to access shared media from iOS and Windows/Mac devices using DLNA. The solution enables wireless media sharing across multiple devices for under $75 total, though it requires some initial configuration and user training.
key_points: The HooToo TripMate Titan at $40 combines external battery, WiFi USB 3.0 media sharing, wired-to-WiFi conversion, and WiFi bridging in one device
For compact high-capacity storage, budget M.2 SSDs in USB 3.0 enclosures dramatically outperform large USB flash drives
DLNA sharing requires no login and works seamlessly with apps like Infuse on iOS
The device has a quirk where the battery slowly drains even when powered off if a USB drive is attached
Simple solutions beat fancy ones - basic DLNA sharing is more practical for family use than complex Plex setups
money_quotes: All this for only forty bucks!
They run absolute circles around large USB flash drives in performance!
The larger the drive, believe me, the more you need to care about this, like say you need to quickly copy a bunch of reasonably new media for the kids to enjoy before you go catch that plane.
With DLNA, no login is required!
But in my book, simple is good.
no matter where you go, there your… files… are.
key_insight: A cheap WiFi storage device plus a flash drive provides a simple, effective solution for sharing media across multiple devices during family travel without expensive hardware upgrades.
